title: Compositions and methods for modulating endoplasmic reticulum-associated degradation
abstract: An isolated polypeptide comprises the amino acid sequence X 5 X 6 ESLQ ERX 13 QALYEYA X 21 RX 23 X 24 (residues 5-24 of SEQ ID NO:1), wherein X 5 , X 6 , X 13 , X 21 , X 23 , and X 24 are each independently selected from any amino acid. Fusion proteins and conjugates of the polypeptide, and methods of making and using the polypeptide, fusion protein, or conjugate are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims 1. An isolated polypeptide comprising the amino acid sequence X 5 X 6 ESLQ ERX 13 QALYEYA X 21 RX 23 X 24 (residues 5-24 of SEQ ID NO:1) wherein X 5, X 6 , X 13 , X 21 , X 23 , and X 24 are each independently selected from any amino acid; preferably wherein X 5 is R, A, E, K, or H; X 6 is Q, A, H, E, K, or R; X 13 is K, A, E, R, or H;X 21 is G, V, L, I, F, D, R, A, or E; X 23 is G, V, L, I, F, D, R, A, or E; and X 24 is F or Y; or an amino acid sequence having at least 85% identity to residues 5-24 of SEQ ID NO:1; with the proviso that the amino acid sequence does not have the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:2. 2. The isolated polypeptide of claim 1, wherein the amino acid sequence is selected from the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:3 (AAESLQERKQALYEYARRRF); the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:4 (RQESLQERKQALYEYAARAF); the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:5 (RQESLQERKQALYEYAARRF); and the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:6 (RQESLQERKQALYEYARRAF). 3. The isolated polypeptide of claim 1 or 2, wherein the polypeptide is bound to a label, a solid support, a lipid monolayer, a heterologous polypeptide sequence, or a combination thereof. 4. The isolated polypeptide of claim 3, wherein the label comprises a radiolabel, a fluorescent label, a chemiluminescent label, an enzymic label, an immunogenic label, or a combination thereof. 5. The isolated polypeptide of claim 3 or 4, wherein the heterologous polypeptide sequence comprises a cell-penetrating peptide sequence. 6. The isolated polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 5 comprising a stapled polypeptide. 7. A fusion protein or conjugate comprising a first moiety, and 

 a second moiety, wherein the first moiety consists of a G2BR domain consisting of the amino acid sequence X 5 X 6 ESLQ ERX 13 QALYEYA X 21 RX 23 X 24 (residues 5-24 of SEQ ID NO:1) wherein X 5, X 6 , X 13 , X 21 , X 23 , and X 24 are each independently selected from any amino acid; preferably wherein X 5 is R, A, E, K, or H; X 6 is Q, A, H, E, K, or R; X 13 is K, A, E, R, or H;X 21 is G, V, L, I, F, D, R, A, or E; X 23 is G, V, L, I, F, D, R, A, or E; and X 24 is F or Y; or an amino acid sequence having at least 85% identity to residues 5-24 of SEQ ID NO:1. 8. The fusion protein or conjugate of claim 7, wherein the amino acid sequence of the G2BR domain is selected from the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:2 (RQESLQERKQALYEYARRRF); the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:3 (AAESLQERKQALYEYARRRF); the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:4 (RQESLQERKQALYEYAARAF); the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:5 (RQESLQERKQALYEYAARRF); and the sequence of residues 5 to 24 of SEQ ID NO:6 (RQESLQERKQALYEYARRAF). 9. The fusion protein or conjugate of claim 7 or 8, wherein the second moiety comprises a label, a solid support, a lipid monolayer, a heterologous polypeptide sequence, or a combination thereof. 10. The fusion protein or conjugate of claim 9, wherein the label comprises a radiolabel, a fluorescent label, a chemiluminescent label, an enzymic label, an immunogenic label, or a combination thereof. 11. The fusion protein or conjugate of claim 9, wherein the heterologous polypeptide sequence comprises a cell-penetrating peptide sequence. 12. The fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 9 to 11 wherein the heterologous polypeptide sequence further comprises an amino acid linker. 13. The fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 8 to 12 comprising a stapled polypeptide. 14. An isolated polynucleotide encoding the isolated polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 6 or the fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 7 to 13. 15. A recombinant vector comprising the isolated polynucleotide of claim 14. 16. A host cell transfected with the vector of claim 15 or the polynucleotide of claim 14. 17. A method of detecting the ubiquitin conjugating enzyme E2G2 (UBE2G2) in a sample, wherein the method comprises: contacting a sample with the polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 6 or the fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 7 to 13; and determining binding of the polypeptide, fusion protein, or conjugate to the UBE2G2 in the sample to detect the presence of UBE2G2 in the sample. 18. A method of modulating expression of a protein in a cell, comprising delivering the polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 6 or the fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 7 to 13 to a eukaryotic cell expressing a protein under conditions such that expression of the protein is modulated. 19. A method of modulating degradation of a protein in a cell, comprising delivering the polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 6 or the fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 7 to 13 to a eukaryotic cell comprising a protein under conditions such that degradation of the protein is modulated. 20. The method of claim 18 or 19, wherein the modulation is an increase. 21. The method of claim 18 or 19, wherein the modulation is a decrease. 22. The method of any one of claims 19 to 21, wherein degradation is decreased by reducing ubiquitination of the protein. 23. A method of reducing degradation of an over-expressed protein in a cell, comprising delivering the polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 6 or the fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 7 to 13 to a eukaryotic cell expressing a protein under conditions such that degradation of the protein is reduced. 24. The method of any one of claims 18 to 23, wherein the protein is an enzyme, a cytokine, an antibody, a receptor, or a therapeutic polypeptide. 25. The method of any one of claims 18 to 24, wherein the protein is an endoplasmic reticulum-associated degradation (ERAD) substrate targeted for ubiquitination by UBE2G2 and an ERAD E3 ubiquitin ligase that uses UBE2G2 as an E2 enzyme. 26. A method of inducing cell death, comprising delivering the polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 6 or the fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 7 to 13 to a eukaryotic cell under conditions such that endoplasmic reticulum- associated degradation (ERAD) of proteins is inhibited and cell death is induced. 27. The method of any one of claims 18 to 26, wherein the cell is a cancer cell. 28. A composition comprising the polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 6, the fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 7 to 13, the polynucleotide of claim 14, or the vector of claim 15 for treatment of an endoplasmic reticulum-associated degradation (ERAD)-associated disorder. 29. The composition of claim 28, wherein the ERAD-associated disorder is cystic fibrosis or cancer. 30. A method of treating an endoplasmic reticulum-associated degradation (ERAD)- associated disorder in a patient comprising 

 administering the polypeptide of any one of claims 1 to 6, the fusion protein or conjugate of any one of claims 7 to 13, the polynucleotide of claim 14, or the vector of claim 15 to a patient having an ERAD-associated disorder. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the ERAD-associated disorder is cystic fibrosis or cancer.
